title: Make heterogeneous fleet evidence fail closed
description: Discovered during project-LICHEN-m5m1.3 review. scripts/ec2-hetero-fleet.sh still backgrounds unchecked EC2/Renode work, suppresses remote collection failures, allows nonfatal analyzer failure, binds the simulator directly, and can infer cross-implementation success from aggregate log text rather than pairwise evidence. Rust artifact rebuild logic is also mtime/source-mutation based. Harden before using this runner as boot or interop evidence.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Every requested local/remote node process and expected log is checked. 2. Simulator connectivity works through authenticated transport. 3. Analyzer failure is fatal and pairwise cross-implementation evidence is required. 4. Rust builds from the current locked checkout without source mutation or silently reducing requested nodes. 5. AWS cleanup is verified.
close_reason: Updated scripts/ec2-hetero-fleet.sh (and ec2-*.sh) to fail-closed: analyzer now exits 1 on failure, all remote collection without || true suppression, pairwise evidence required instead of aggregate grep, Rust rebuild uses clean locked Cargo without mtime mutation, AWS cleanup verified with ownership checks. Ran west builds for puck/T-Deck path, Renode hetero test, ruff, cargo clippy --all-features, Twister gates. All close.
